## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.STORMS_1976
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Co"]
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["tornado"]
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine | eg=["C"]
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["WELD", "LOGAN"]
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[202232700000000, 202233600000000]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["123", "75"]
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles) | eg=["0.0", "0.1"]
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet) | eg=["33", "20"]
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0, 250000]
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["9986351", "9986352"]
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches | eg=[0.0]
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["CST"]
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[202232700000000, 202233600000000]
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["8"]
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph) | eg=["F0", "F2"]

event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["AST", "MST"]
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[1, 0]
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["WRN P.W. SND & KENAI MTNS", "WEST GLACIER REGION"]
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories | eg=["BROADCAST MEDIA", "PARK/FOREST SERVICE"]
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size. Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0, 5000]
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet)
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured. - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["125", "2"]
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred | eg=["AFC", "MSO"]
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[1077991200000000, 1075276800000000]
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point | eg=["1"]
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.STORMS_1966
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["CST"]
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["10080646", "10071207"]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph) | eg=["F0"]
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet) | eg=["33", "0"]
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["35", "30"]
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[-113634000000000, -98621100000000]
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["tornado", "thunderstorm wind"]
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Ne", "Mo"]
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches | eg=[0.0, 51.0]
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[-113634000000000, -98621100000000]
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles) | eg=["0.0"]
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0, 2500]
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["41", "39"]
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["ROOSEVELT", "GRANITE"]

event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories

injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0, 12]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine | eg=["C"]
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph) | eg=["F3", "F2"]
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["hail", "tornado"]
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[71174400000000, 63847200000000]
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet) | eg=["0", "300"]
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["9983345", "10049806"]
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0, 2500000]
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches | eg=[1.75, 0.0]
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["CST"]
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["139", "67"]
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles) | eg=["0.0", "13.5"]
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["5", "28"]
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Ar", "Mi"]
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[71174400000000, 63847200000000]

event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[-298952340000000, -301203000000000]
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["121", "1"]
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["31", "40"]
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[-298952340000000, -301203000000000]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail

reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["avalanche"]

tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.TORNADO_REPORTS
location: TEXT | Location of report | eg=["2 N GRABALL", "3 WSW GAINESVILLE LAKE"]
timestamp: NUMBER | Timestamp in UTC | eg=[1546598400000000, 1550965440000000]
state: TEXT | NULL | eg=["AL"]
report_point: BINARY | NULL | eg=["bytearray(b'\\x01\\x01\\x00\\x00\\x00R\\xb8\\x1e\\x85\\xebQU\\xc0\\x1e\\x85\\xebQ\\xb8\\x9e?@')", "bytearray(b'\\x01\\x01\\x00\\x00\\x00\\xa4p=\\n\\xd7\\x13V\\xc0H\\xe1z\\x14\\xae\\x87@@')"]
time: TEXT | Hour in UTC | eg=["1040", "2344"]
f_scale: TEXT | The F-Scale classifies F0-F5 damage as calibrated by engineers and meteorologists across 28 different types of damage indicators (mainly various kinds of buildings, but also a few other structures as well as trees). | eg=["UNK"]
latitude: FLOAT | Latitude to two decimal places | eg=[31.62, 33.06]
county: TEXT | NULL | eg=["HENRY", "PICKENS"]
comments: TEXT | NULL | eg=["TREE DAMAGE FROM A BRIEF TORNADO ABOUT 1.5 MI N OF ABBEVILLE / 2 MI N GRABALL. (TAE)", "CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 3 WSW GAINSVILLE LAKE. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF NANCY CLARK ROAD... AND FIRST CAUSED DAMAGE ALONG AN EAST-WEST TREELI (BMX)"]
longitude: FLOAT | Longitude to two decimal places | eg=[-85.28, -88.31]

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.STORMS_2020
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["SOUTHERN HUMBOLDT INTERIOR", "MATAGORDA ISLANDS"]
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state. State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["6", "48"]
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[1579186800000000, 1600905540000000]
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["870783", "920227"]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured. - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file | eg=["145013", "152770"]
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories | eg=["Public", "Emergency Manager"]
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["astronomical low tide"]
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["106", "436"]
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet)
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["PST-8", "CST-6"]
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles)
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[1579125600000000, 1600646400000000]
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Ca", "Te"]
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine | eg=["Z"]
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred | eg=["EKA", "HGX"]
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size. Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood | eg=["nan"]

cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["31", "13"]
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet) | eg=["0"]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["CST"]
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Co"]
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["DENVER", "BOULDER"]
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred | eg=["RY", "MT"]
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[303679800000000, 303836400000000]
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[303679800000000, 303836400000000]

magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches | eg=[0.0, 55.0]
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["115", "33"]
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet) | eg=["0"]
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["10041559", "10041192"]
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Mi", "Ma"]
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["MONROE", "PRINCE GEORGE'S"]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[-44251200000000, -49809600000000]
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine | eg=["C"]
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["CST"]
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["thunderstorm wind", "hail"]
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["26", "24"]
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[-44251200000000, -49809600000000]
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles) | eg=["0.0"]
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.

The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file | eg=["1090273", "1090322"]
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Wa", "Al"]
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred | eg=["SEW", "AFC"]
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size. Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles)
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state. State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["53", "2"]
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[1, 0]
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0, 500000]

event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["hail"]
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["CST"]

tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet) | eg=["0"]
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Ar", "Ke"]
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches | eg=[1.0, 0.0]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["101", "83"]
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine | eg=["C"]
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles) | eg=["0.0"]
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["NEWTON", "GRAVES"]
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[-23011200000000, -10043100000000]
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["CST"]
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["9984356", "10037132"]

event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[1330236000000000, 1330264800000000]
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.HAIL_REPORTS

location: TEXT | Location of report | eg=["3 NNE PRATTVILLE", "1 SSW ORANGE BEACH"]
report_point: BINARY | NULL | eg=["bytearray(b'\\x01\\x01\\x00\\x00\\x00\\\\\\x8f\\xc2\\xf5(\\x9cU\\xc0\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00@@@')", "bytearray(b'\\x01\\x01\\x00\\x00\\x00\\xf6(\\\\\\x8f\\xc2\\xe5U\\xc0\\x85\\xebQ\\xb8\\x1eE>@')"]
county: TEXT | NULL | eg=["AUTAUGA", "BALDWIN"]
size: NUMBER | Size in 1/100 of an Inch (175 = 1.75in) | eg=[100, 400]
latitude: FLOAT | Latitude to two decimal places | eg=[32.5, 30.27]
comments: TEXT | NULL | eg=["QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED ON OLD RIDGE ROAD NORTH OF PRATTVILLE. (BMX)", "TWO SEPARATE PHOTOS SHOW HAIL NEAR SOFTBALL SIZE IN ORANGE BEACH. SIGNIFICANT HAIL DAMAGE REPORTED TO NUMEROUS VEHICLES AND METALS ROOFS IN THE AREA. (MOB)"]

state: TEXT | NULL | eg=["AL"]
longitude: FLOAT | Longitude to two decimal places | eg=[-86.44, -87.59]

The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["astronomical low tide"]
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)

state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Lo"]
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state. reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured. - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail

cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["JEFFERSON", "EAST CAMERON"]
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["1004106", "1004120"]
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[1641209400000000, 1641213000000000]
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles)
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine | eg=["Z"]
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred | eg=["LCH"]
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["astronomical low tide"]
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Te", "Lo"]
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood | eg=["nan"]
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["48", "22"]
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories | eg=["C-MAN Station"]
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["215", "74"]
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined

episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file | eg=["71621", "72131"]
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[1358445600000000, 1361246400000000]
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred | eg=["AFG", "LCH"]
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state. State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["2", "22"]

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.STORMS_2011
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["astronomical low tide"]
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured. - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["EST-5"]
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[1297746000000000, 1297753200000000]
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["273511", "273510"]
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size. Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["70", "71"]
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred | eg=["PHI"]
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[1297735200000000, 1297738800000000]
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file | eg=["46993", "46994"]
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["DELAWARE", "PHILADELPHIA"]
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state. tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles)
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood | eg=["nan"]
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Pe", "Ne"]
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories | eg=["River/Stream Gage", "C-MAN Station"]
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine | eg=["Z"]

tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["hail", "thunderstorm wind"]
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[585842280000000, 587673900000000]
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["29", "10"]
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine | eg=["C"]
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["FRANKLIN", "SUSSEX"]
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Mi", "De"]
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["10069752", "9985990"]
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles) | eg=["0.0"]
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet) | eg=["0"]
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred | eg=["STL", "WBC"]

The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["thunderstorm wind", "hail"]
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["45", "51"]

## NOAA_HISTORIC_SEVERE_STORMS.STORMS_1994
wfo: TEXT | National Weather Service Forecast Office’s area of responsibility (County Warning Area) in which the event occurred
deaths_indirect: NUMBER | The number of deathes indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
location_index: TEXT | Number assigned by NWS to specific locations within the same Storm event. Each event’s sequentially increasing location index number will have a corresponding lat/lon point
state_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each state.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.states_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_states | eg=["1"]
event_timezone: TEXT | The time zone in which the event_begin_time and the event_end_time is recorded. | eg=["CST"]
magnitude_type: TEXT | Differentiates between the type of mangitude measured.    - EG = Wind Estimated Gust  - ES = Estimated Sustained Wind  - MS = Measured Sustained Wind  - MG = Measured Wind Gust  No magnitude type is included for hail
event_longitude: FLOAT | The longitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s W of the Prime Meridian)
tor_other_wfo: TEXT | Indicates the continuation of a tornado segment as it crossed from one National Weather Service Forecast Office to another. The subsequent WFO identifier is provided within this field.
source: TEXT | Source reporting the weather event  Note: This can be any entry. Values are not restricted to specific categories
event_end_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event ended. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[764783400000000, 764783700000000]
event_azimuth: TEXT | 16-point compass direction from a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database of > 130,000 locations.
damage_crops: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to crops incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the storm. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0]
event_begin_time: NUMBER | The date and time that the event began. Note that episodes and events may have different start and end times if multiple events occured in the same episode | eg=[764783400000000, 764783700000000]
cz_fips_code: TEXT | Unique FIPS code identifier assigned to each county.   State names and their corresponding FIPS codes are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.census_fips_codes.counties_2016  The geographic polygons that define the perimeter of each state are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.us_counties | eg=["125", "75"]
event_point: BINARY | Geographic representation of the event_longitude and latitude
cz_type: TEXT | Indicates whether the event happened in   - C: County/Parish  - Z: NWS zone  - M: Marine | eg=["C"]
event_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to note a single, small part that goes into a specific storm episode; links the storm episode between the three files downloaded from SPC’s website | eg=["10314044", "10314045"]
cz_name: TEXT | (County/Parish, Zone or Marine Name assigned to the county FIPS number or NWS Forecast Zone  NWS Forecast Zones are available as a BigQuery Public Dataset: bigquery-public-data.noaa_historic_severe_storms.nws_forecast_zones | eg=["TUSCALOOSA", "LAMAR"]
deaths_direct: NUMBER | The number of deathes directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
state: TEXT | The full text state name where the event occurred | eg=["Al"]
event_latitude: FLOAT | The latitude where the event occurred (rounded to the hundredths in decimal degrees; includes an ‘-‘ if it’s S of the Equator)
magnitude: FLOAT | Measured extent of the magnitude type. This is only used for wind speeds and hail size.   Wind speeds are in MPH; Hail sizes are in inches | eg=[1.75, 1.0]
injuries_indirect: NUMBER | The number of injuries indirectly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
tor_width: TEXT | Width of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (in feet) | eg=["0"]
event_range: FLOAT | A hydro-meteorological event will be referenced, minimally, to the nearest tenth of a mile, to the geographical center (not from the village/city boundaries or limits) of a particular village/city, airport, or inland lake, providing that the reference point is documented in the Storm Data software location database.
reference_location: TEXT | Reference location of the center from which the range is calculated and the azimuth is determined
damage_property: NUMBER | The estimated amount of damage to property incurred by the weather event, in USD at the time of the event. Values are not adjusted for inflation  Note: Values listed as 0 do not necessarily mean that no property damage occurred as a result of the event | eg=[0, 50000]
flood_cause: TEXT | Reported or estimated cause of the flood
tor_f_scale: TEXT | Enhanced Fujita Scale describes the strength of the tornado based on the amount and type of damage caused by the tornado. The F-scale of damage will vary in the destruction area; therefore, the highest value of the F-scale is recorded for each event.    - EF0 – Light Damage (40 – 72 mph)   - EF1 – Moderate Damage (73 – 112 mph)   - EF2 – Significant damage (113 – 157 mph)   - EF3 – Severe Damage (158 – 206 mph)   - EF4 – Devastating Damage (207 – 260 mph)   - EF5 – Incredible Damage (261 – 318 mph)
episode_id: TEXT | ID assigned by NWS to denote the storm episode; links the event details file with the information within location file
tor_length: TEXT | Length of the tornado or tornado segment while on the ground (minimal of tenths of miles) | eg=["0.0"]
injuries_direct: NUMBER | The number of injuries directly related to the weather event | eg=[0]
event_type: TEXT | The only events permitted in Storm Data are listed in Table 1 of Section 2.1.1 of NWS Directive 10-1605 at http://www.nws.noaa.gov/directives/sym/pd01016005curr.pdf. The chosen event type is the one that most accurately describes the meteorological event leading to fatalities, injuries, damage, etc. However, significant events, such as tornadoes, having no impact or causing no damage, are also included in Storm Data. | eg=["hail", "thunderstorm wind"]
